goal: Introduce a reusable employee UI port and pluggable UI adapters for normal web clients, boundless employee-as-website experiments, embedded surfaces, UI Lab, and future MCP Apps while allowing reusable layouts, profile-generated themes, and explicit custom overrides.
non_goals: Do not deploy a public employee homepage.
Do not add a database migration.
Do not replace the current operating-state model or renderer tree.
Do not add a visual design framework or heavyweight optimization dependency.
Do not claim browser screenshots or production evidence when execution is unavailable.
authority: current shared, Manager, Web, UI Lab, onboarding, profile and browser-test source
observations: The repository already has one operating projection compiler, one layout planner, one renderer registry and one production AgentSurface.
Employee profile key/version, business kind, dominant domains, owner experience and density already reach the operating context.
Onboarding captures branding facts but has no first-class UI presentation profile.
Talk and Workspace styling is largely component-local and hard-coded.
UI Lab already routes fixtures through production AgentSurface but cannot select adapter/layout/theme combinations from the route.
The existing browser harness can render screenshots but does not define an adapter matrix with adapter-specific provenance.
unknowns: Visual quality of new adapter combinations until Playwright runs in a dependency-complete environment.
Which custom public employee surfaces will be productized first.
Whether MCP Apps should consume this exact visual registry or only the same UI port contract.
